skill.name: twilio-communications
skill.description: Build communication features with Twilio: SMS messaging, voice calls, WhatsApp Business API, and user verification (2FA).
skill.summary: Build communication features with Twilio: SMS messaging, voice calls, WhatsApp Business API, and user verification with secure credential handling.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings were evaluated as false positives. The skill uses the official Twilio Python SDK with security best practices: credentials stored in environment variables, request signature validation for webhooks, proper exception handling, and phone number validation. No malicious patterns or security vulnerabilities detected.

evidence.reason: Python Twilio SDK imports, not shell execution
Flask app with Twilio webhook validation
Official Twilio RequestValidator for webhook security
Secure credential handling via environment variables

content.value_statement: Add SMS, voice calls, WhatsApp messaging, and 2FA to your applications using Twilio. This skill handles phone verification, IVR systems, and secure credential management with the official Twilio SDK.
actual_capabilities: Send SMS messages with delivery status tracking and E.164 phone validation
Implement 2FA and OTP verification using Twilio Verify service
Build IVR phone menu systems with TwiML voice responses
Validate Twilio webhook signatures for secure request handling
Support multiple communication channels: SMS, voice, WhatsApp, email
Handle Twilio API errors with proper exception management
limitations: Requires valid Twilio account with API credentials configured
SMS messages limited to 160 characters per segment (additional cost for longer)
Voice IVR systems require publicly accessible webhook URLs for Twilio callbacks
A2P 10DLC registration required for US business SMS compliance

use_cases.title: E-commerce Order Notifications
use_cases.description: Send order confirmations, shipping updates, and delivery notifications to customers via SMS.
use_cases.target_user: E-commerce developers building customer notification systems

use_cases.title: User Authentication with 2FA
use_cases.description: Add phone-based two-factor authentication to protect user accounts from unauthorized access.

use_cases.title: Automated Customer Support Phone System
use_cases.description: Create IVR menus that route calls to appropriate departments and handle after-hours voicemail.
use_cases.target_user: Small business owners needing professional phone systems

prompt_templates.title: Send a Basic SMS Notification
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me send an SMS message to +1234567890 with the text 'Your order has shipped'. I have Twilio credentials set up as environment variables.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Phone Verification for User Signup
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to verify user phone numbers during signup. Create a flow that sends a verification code via SMS and validates the code the user enters.

prompt_templates.title: Build an IVR Phone Menu System
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Flask app with TwiML that answers calls with a greeting, collects digit input (press 1 for sales, 2 for support), and routes to the correct department.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Full IVR system with call routing

prompt_templates.title: Add WhatsApp Business Messaging
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up WhatsApp messaging for customer support using Twilio. I need to send template messages and handle incoming replies with proper webhook validation.

output_examples.input: Send SMS to +15551234567 with order confirmation
output_examples.output: Message sent successfully. SID: SM1234abcd5678efgh. Status: queued. Segments: 1
Verification approved. Status: approved. Valid: true

output_examples.input: Handle incoming call with IVR menu
output_examples.output: XML response generated with voice greeting and digit collection. Caller pressed 2, routed to support line.

best_practices: Store Twilio credentials (Account SID, Auth Token) in environment variables, never in code
Validate Twilio webhook signatures using RequestValidator to prevent spoofed requests
Implement rate limiting at application level in addition to Twilio's built-in limits
Track user opt-out status to comply with SMS marketing regulations
Use E.164 format for all phone numbers to ensure reliable delivery
anti_patterns: Hardcoding API credentials directly in source code or configuration files
Skipping webhook signature validation on incoming Twilio requests
Sending messages to users without tracking consent and opt-out preferences
Ignoring message segmentation costs for long SMS content

faq.question: How do I get Twilio API credentials?
faq.answer: Sign up at twilio.com, then find your Account SID and Auth Token in the Console dashboard. Create a phone number for sending messages.

faq.question: What is E.164 format and why is it required?
faq.answer: E.164 is the international phone number format: +[country code][number], like +14155551234. Twilio requires this format for reliable routing.

faq.question: How does Twilio Verify differ from building my own OTP system?
faq.answer: Twilio Verify handles code generation, rate limiting, fraud prevention, and multi-channel delivery automatically. Building your own requires managing all security aspects.

faq.question: What is A2P 10DLC registration?
faq.answer: A2P 10DLC is required for US business SMS. It registers your brand and campaign with carriers to improve deliverability and avoid filtering.

faq.question: How do I handle SMS opt-outs?
faq.answer: Track user preferences in your database. When users reply STOP, mark them as opted-out and exclude from future campaigns to comply with regulations.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for voice calls to any country?
faq.answer: Twilio supports voice calls to most countries, but rates and regulations vary. Check Twilio's pricing page for destination-specific rates and requirements.
